coredata.dc:title: Colorimetric detection of biothiols and Hg2+ based on the peroxidase-like activity of GTP
coredata.prism:publicationName: Spectrochimica Acta Part A: Molecular and Biomolecular Spectroscopy
coredata.dc:creator: Huang, Na
Yang, Dan
Chen, Huanhuan
Xiao, Yu
Wen, Jiahui
Long, Yijuan
Zheng, Huzhi
coredata.dc:description: Guanosine-5′-triphosphate (GTP) not only plays a key role in a majority of cellular processes but also be proposed as a peroxidase-like mimic. Compared with nanozymes, GTP shows good tolerance under harsh conditions, which can be used to construct an easy colorimetric analysis for the detection of biomolecules. Here, on the basis of the peroxidase-like activity of GTP which can catalyze the oxidation of 3,3′,5,5′-tetramethyl benzidine dihydrochloride (TMB), colorimetric sensing was established for biothiols and Hg2+. Biothiols reduced the oxTMB back to colorless TMB, and Hg2+ restored the formation of oxTMB, leading to the recovery of color. This method not only provides a platform for the detection of metal ions and biothiols, but also shows that GTP has great potential for analytical detection.
coredata.dcterms:subject: GTP
Peroxidase-like activity
Biothiols
Hg2+

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Detecting L-Cys and Hg2+ in really samples
Feasibility of detecting biothiols and Hg2+
Colorimetric method for biothiols and Hg2+ detection
Detection of L-Cys and Hg2+ in real samples

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: Absorption spectra of (a) TMB + H2O2; (b) GTP + TMB + H2O2; (c) GTP + TMB + H2O2 + L-Cys; (d) GTP + TMB + H2O2 + L-Cys + Hg2+; (e); GTP + TMB + H2O2 + Hg2+. TMB: 0.80 mM, GTP:1.0 μM; H2O2: 2.0 mM and 20.0 mM PBS (pH 4.0) buffer solution was used for chromogenic reactions.
Optimization of related conditions (a) pH; (b) reaction temperature; (c) H2O2 concentration; (d) reaction time. (e) GTP concentration.

Absorption spectra of determination system with different concentrations of (a) L-Cys, (c) GSH, (e) Hg2+.Linear relationship plot of (b) L-Cys, (d) GSH, (f) Hg2+. TMB: 0.80 mM, GTP:1.0 μM; H2O2: 2.0 mM and 20.0 mM PBS (pH 4.0) buffer solution was used for chromogenic reaction.

Selectivity for L-Cys and Hg2+ detection. Selectivity test for (a) L-Cys and (c) Hg2+ detecting with different substances; Effects of coexisting substances for detecting (b) 10.0 μM L-Cys and (d) 5.0 μM Hg2+. TMB: 0.80 mM, GTP:1.0 μM; H2O2: 2.0 mM and 20.0 mM PBS (pH 4.0) buffer solution was used for chromogenic reaction. (10.0 μM for L-Cys, GSH, Hcy, 100.0 μM for Thr, Ser, Val, His, Phe, Asn, Ala, Ile, Gln, Glu, Asp, Lys, Trp, Leu, Gly, Met, Pro, Tyr, Arg); (5.0 μM for Hg2+ and Ag+, 50.0 μM for Na+, Mg2+, K+, Zn2+, Co2+, Ca2+, Cr3+, Ba2+, 25.0 μM for Mn2+, Cd2+).
Schematic illustration for detecting biothiols and Hg2+.
Detection of L-Cys in really FBS samples.
Detection of Hg2+ in really samples.

head.ce:title: Colorimetric detection of biothiols and Hg2+ based on the peroxidase-like activity of GTP

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: College of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering, Southwest University, Beibei, Chongqing 400715, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: College of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: College of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering, Southwest University, Beibei, Chongqing 400715, China

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: This scientifically describes a simple, cost-effective, and sensitive colorimetric method for the quantitative detection of biothiols and Hg2+ based on the peroxidase-like activity of GTP.

Guanosine-5′-triphosphate (GTP) not only plays a key role in a majority of cellular processes but also be proposed as a peroxidase-like mimic. Compared with nanozymes, GTP shows good tolerance under harsh conditions, which can be used to construct an easy colorimetric analysis for the detection of biomolecules. Here, on the basis of the peroxidase-like activity of GTP which can catalyze the oxidation of 3,3′,5,5′-tetramethyl benzidine dihydrochloride (TMB), colorimetric sensing was established for biothiols and Hg2+. Biothiols reduced the oxTMB back to colorless TMB, and Hg2+ restored the formation of oxTMB, leading to the recovery of color. This method not only provides a platform for the detection of metal ions and biothiols, but also shows that GTP has great potential for analytical detection.

ce:data-availability.ce:para: Data will be made available on request.

ce:section.ce:para: Biothiols such as L-cysteine(L-Cys), homocysteine (Hcy) and glutathione (GSH) contain an active sulfhydryl group (-SH) in their chemical structures and are readily dehydrogenated [1–3]. They are vital antioxidants against oxidative stress and related to many physiological and pathological processes [4,5]. In addition, L-Cys can produce GSH in the presence of glutathione synthase (GS), and fluctuations of either side have significant or complex effects on other substances, leading to serious health problems [6,7]. Hg2+ are highly poisonous heavy metals, which may damage kidneys, brain, nervous and endocrine systems at excessive levels [8,9]. Therefore, it is fully significant to detect the concentration of biothiols and Hg2+ quickly and accurately in many pathological processes [10]. Over the past decades, some methods have been developed for detecting these metal ions and small biological molecules, such as high-performance liquid chromatography [11], electrochemical analysis [12], fluorescent-based [13–15] and colorimetric methods [16,17]. Among them, colorimetric methods have attracted considerable attention because of their speed, low cost and simple operation.

The most reported colorimetric strategies are based on the catalytic activity of artificial enzymes, which has aroused a lot of interest due to its highly active and easy preparation. The nanozymes such as gold nanoparticles [18,19], Fe3O4 nanoparticles [20], CuO nanoparticles [21,22], carbon dots [23], and metal–organic frameworks [24,25] have been used to detect biomolecule through colorimetric sensing. Nevertheless, the activities of the nanomaterials obtained from various batches are different, and the nanomaterials tend to aggregate [26,27]. Now, more and more small molecules have been discovered to show enzyme-like activity, which can address the above drawbacks apparently [28,29].

As one of the four essential ribonucleotides for RNA synthesis, GTP plays a key role in many important biological processes in cells, which is an energy source for translation and other important cellular processes and undertakes the important role in transcription [30–32]. In addition to the above functions of GTP, we also identified that it exhibited a comparatively high peroxidase-like activity [33], which can be used to develop colorimetric sensing.

GTP can catalyze the oxidation of colorless substrate to color products in the presence of H2O2. In this paper, taking advantage of the peroxidase-like activity of GTP, we have developed a simple and rapid method for the detection of biothiols and Hg2+. The detection mechanism was demonstrated in Scheme 1 , GTP was a commercial product purchased directly without further treatment, catalyzing the oxidation of TMB to develop the chromogenic reaction. Whereas, the process of chromogenic reaction was prevented due to the adding of boithiols, which inhibit the generation of oxTMB. On the contrary, addition of Hg2+ recovers the production of oxTMB and the chromogenic reaction continues to depend on the specific binding between L-Cys and Hg2+. As a consequence, both Hg2+ and biothiols can be detected simply and accurately.

Biothiols were detected as follows: H2O (500 μL), pH 4.0 phosphate buffered saline (PBS, 100 μL, 200.0 mM), GTP (100 μL, 10.0 μM), TMB (100 μL, 8.0 mM), H2O2 (100 μL, 20.0 mM) were mixed with different concentrations of biothiols (100 μL) in a 2.0 mL centrifuge tube. Finally, the absorbance was recorded and analyzed using a UV-2450 spectrophotometer (Shimadzu, Japan) after incubating at 50℃ for 30 min.

Hg2+ was detected as follows: At first, L-Cys (100 μL, 200.0 μM) mixed with different concentrations of Hg2+ in a 2.0 mL centrifuge tube and incubated at 37 ℃ for 30 min. Next, H2O (500 μL), PBS (pH 4.0, 100 μL, 200.0 mM), GTP (100 μL, 10.0 μM), TMB (100 μL, 8.0 mM), H2O2 (100 μL, 20.0 mM) was added to the centrifuge tube. Then the mixed solution was reacted at 50℃ for 30 min. At last, the absorbance at 652 nm was recorded.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Detecting L-Cys and Hg2+ in really samples
ce:section.ce:para: The concentrations of L-Cys in fetalbovineserum (FBS) were determined by the standard addition method. The FBS samples were centrifuged for 30 min with a 3000KD ultrafiltration tube at low speed to obtain the purified samples with deproteinization. The collected filtrate was then diluted 20 times with ultra-pure water for subsequent use. 400 μL of H2O, 100 μL of PBS (pH 4.0), 100 μL of GTP (10.0 μM), 100 μL of TMB (8.0 mM), 100 μL of H2O2 (20.0 mM) and different concentrations of L-Cys mixed with 100 μL of diluted serum in a 2.0 mL centrifuge tube. Finally, absorption spectra were measured.

The detection of Hg2+ in real samples was also tested by the standard addition method. Tap water obtained from the laboratory was filtered with 0.22 μm microfiltration membrane and then diluted 10 times for use. Next, 100 μL of diluted tap water and 100 μL of L-Cys (200.0 μM) were added to 100 μL of Hg2+ with different concentrations, and then, reacted at 37 ℃ for 30 min. After that, 100 μL of PBS (pH 4.0), 100 μL of GTP (10.0 μM), 100 μL of TMB (8.0 mM), 100 μL of H2O2 (20.0 mM) were introduced to the mixture and the final volume of solution was 1.0 mL adjusted with ultra-pure water. The absorbance was measured by a UV 2450 spectrophotometer after 30 min incubation.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Feasibility of detecting biothiols and Hg2+
ce:section.ce:para: GTP, whose high peroxidase-like activity has been investigated, can catalyze the oxidation of colorless TMB to blue oxTMB with the participation of H2O2 [33]. Considering that biothiols are important antioxidants during oxidative stress which could suppress the catalytic oxidation process of GTP, Hg2+ with affinity for biothiols because of the formation of Hg-S- bond [34] which could restore catalyze the oxidation of colorless TMB to blue product. On the basis of the above, the concentrations of biothiols and Hg2+ can be quantitatively detected by measuring the absorbance of the sensing system. As shown in Fig. 1 , the absorbance of GTP + TMB + H2O2 (line b) was much higher than TMB + H2O2, indicating the intrinsic peroxidase-like activity of GTP. However, the absorbance fell significantly when biothiols were introduced to the GTP + TMB + H2O2 mixture solutions (line c), showing that biothiols could suppress the oxidation of TMB. The line “d” displayed that Hg2+ could restore the color of oxTMB due to the stronger affinity between -SH with Hg2+. What’s more, Hg2+ had no effect on the peroxidase-like activity of GTP (line e), thus the colorimetric sensing for biothiols and Hg2+ was feasible.

The colorimetric sensing for biothiols and Hg2+ also relied on experimental conditions such as pH, temperature, reaction time, the concentration of H2O2 and GTP. To achieve the best detection performance, we have optimized the relevant conditions and the ΔA value was used as the criterion: ΔA = A0–A, where A and A0 are the absorbance of the system in the presence and absence of 10 μM biothiols, respectively. At first, we explored the effect of pH, and Fig. 2 a shows that pH 4.0 of PBS buffer was optimal. Next, the impact of temperature varying from 20 °C to 80 °C was measured and the ΔA reached a peak at 50 °C (Fig. 2b). Consequently, 50 °C was chosen for further experiment. As the concentration of H2O2 increased, the ΔA increased gradually. After the concentration exceeded 2.0 mM, the ΔA reached a plateau (Fig. 2c), so we selected 2.0 mM as the optimal concentration. At the same time, Fig. 2d indicated that 30 min was the best reaction time. The sensitivity of detection strategy was related to the concentration of the mimic enzyme, so, we optimized the concentration of the mimic enzyme. Fig. 2e revealed that the ΔA significantly changes as the concentration of GTP increased from 0.10 μM to 1.0 μM and almost reached a plateau at 1.0 μM to 1.5 μM, then decreased with further increasing of GTP. Hence, 1.0 μM of GTP was used in subsequent research.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Colorimetric method for biothiols and Hg2+ detection
ce:section.ce:para: GTP showed inherent peroxidase activity, which can catalyze the oxidation of chromogenic substrates with the participation of H2O2. Here we chose TMB as chromogenic substrates for colorimetric sensing. Owing to the antioxidant properties of biothiols (L-Cys and GSH), the addition of biothiols in the system can completely or partially inhibit the oxidation of TMB. On this basis, biothiols can be quantitatively analyzed through measuring the change of the absorbance. As illustrated in Fig. 3 a, with the concentration of L-Cys increased, the absorbance at 652 nm decreased accordingly, and a good linear correlation can be obtained within the absorbance at 652 nm and the concentration of L-Cys from 0.10 to 20.0 μM (Fig. 3b). The regression equation was A = −0.0316C + 0.8292 (R2 = 0.9942), and the detection limit (LOD) was calculated to be 0.094 μM (3σ/κ). Meanwhile, the absorbance also fell with the increasing concentration of GSH in Fig. 3c, and it exhibited a satisfactory linear relationship in the range of 0.1–30.0 μM with a LOD of 0.10 μM (Fig. 3d). The regression equation was A = −0.0238C + 0.8396 (R2 = 0.9977). After comparing the detection ranges and LODs of different methods, the strategy that we put forward has exhibited an equal or superior sensitivity for the L-Cys and GSH determination (Table S1). Therefore, biothiols including L-Cys and GSH can be reliably and quantitatively detected utilizing the peroxidase-like activity of GTP.

With the introduction of L-Cys, the generation of oxTMB in the system will be prevented, but Hg2+ showed a strong interaction with L-Cys due to the formation of Hg2+-S. Therefore, a colorimetric method for Hg2+ was built based on restoring the oxidation of TMB. As demonstrated in Fig. 3e, the absorbance is gradually recovering with the increasing concentration of Hg2+, and the absorbance at 652 nm was linearly correlated with Hg2+ concentration varying 0.05 to 10.0 μM (A = 0.07826C + 0.13444; R2 = 0.9994). The LOD was 0.038 μM. GTP has certain advantages compared with other previously reported assays in detection of Hg2+ (Table S2).

We investigated the selectivity of the above detection method for biothiols by studying the response of common interfering substances in biological samples. Among common amino acid (threonine, serine, valine, histidine, phenylalanine, asparagine, alanine, isoleucine, glutamine, glutamate, aspartate, lysine, tryptophan, leucine, glycine, methionine, proline, tyrosine, Arginine) and three types of biothiols (L-Cys, GSH and Hcy), only three kinds of biothiols gave a reduced absorbance value at 652 nm in Fig. 4 a, demonstrating that those interfering substances almost can’t suppressed the oxidation of TMB and presented a negligible effect on the detection of biothiols. To further verify the selectivity of the developed method, we investigated the effect of coexisting substances on detecting L-Cys. As illustrated in Fig. 4b, it is displayed that the related amino acid had no significant impact on the detection of L-Cys even though the concentration of interfering substances was 10-folds higher than that of L-Cys. Considering that the concentrations of L-Cys in actual serum were much higher than GSH and Hcy [11,35], thus L-Cys can be quickly and accurately determined in real sample.

According to the interaction between Hg2+ and L-Cys, Hg2+ can restore the generation of blue products in solution, and Hg2+ can be quantitatively detected. To verify the selectivity of Hg2+ detection, we selected some metal ions (Na+; Mg2+; K+; Zn2+; Co2+; Ca2+; Cr3+; Mn2+; Cd2+; Ba2+; Ag+) for interference experiments (Fig. 4c, d). The change of absorbance at 652 nm was mainly due to the adding of Hg2+ and other metal ions don’t have significant effect on the change of absorbance. What’s more, the response toward Hg2+ was scarcely interfered by the presence of the coexisting interference ions. In summary, our detection platform showed favorable selectivity for Hg2+.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Detection of L-Cys and Hg2+ in real samples
ce:section.ce:para: To verify the practicability of this method in real samples, we applied it to detect L-Cys in fetalbovineserum samples and standard addition method was utilized in this study. As listed in Table 1 , the added scalar was in good agreement with the measured value, and the average recoveries are between 90.4% and 98.3% with RSD in the average of 1.0–2.7%, demonstrating a dependable and practical colorimetric approach.

The practical applicability of the method for determining Hg2+ in tap water samples was also studied. The consequence was shown in Table 2 demonstrating that the recoveries for Hg2+ in the water samples are between 100.6% and 114.7% and RSD between 3.6% and 9.6%. In conclusion, these experimental results suggest that L-Cys and Hg2+ in actual samples can be easily and quickly detected using the peroxidase-like activity of GTP.

In brief, we have successfully constructed a simple, fast and low-cost colorimetric platform for the determination of biothiols and Hg2+. GTP was chosen as a peroxidase-like mimic which overcome the shortcomings of nanomaterials, such as tending to aggregation and unstable. Under optimal conditions, the linear range of L-Cys was 0.10–20.0 μM and the detection limit was 94.0 nM. This assay shows a detection limit of GSH of 100 nM and a linear range of 0.10–30.0 μM. The colorimetric sensors exhibited a satisfactory linear relationship in the range of 0.05–10.0 μM with a LOD of 38.0 nM for Hg2+. In addition, the proposed strategy was applied to the detection of L-Cys in fetal bovine serum and Hg2+ in tap water successfully. In summary, the proposed multifunctional platform was convenient, fast and low-budget, and has broad application prospects in the fields of biosensing and biotechnology.

Na Huang: Conceptualization, Visualization, Methodology, Data curation, Writing – original draft. Dan Yang: Investigation, Writing – review & editing. Huanhuan Chen: Investigation, Software. Yu Xiao: Investigation, Writing – review & editing. Jiahui Wen: Software, Investigation. Yijuan Long: Supervision, Resources. Huzhi Zheng: Supervision, Project administration, Writing – review & editing.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This work was supported by Central Government Funds for Local Science and Technology Development (XZ202102YD0033C) and the Fundamental Research Funds for the Central Universities (XDJK2020C033).

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para: Supplementary data to this article can be found online at https://doi.org/10.1016/j.saa.2022.122263.

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para:
The following are the Supplementary data to this article:
Supplementary data 1
